replication_study.hypothesis: Students with higher psychological distress (Severe Distress category on K10) will have lower odds of higher motivation for distance learning (online_learning_1), controlling for no additional covariates as in the original focal test.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: AlTammemi_Survey_deidentify.csv (Prolific-based replication sample; created cutoff 2021-01-19 17:50:32 applied)
data_plan.source_type: Online survey (panel recruitment via Prolific; de-identified responses)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Complete cases after filtering created timestamp > 2021-01-19 17:50:32 and applying targeted imputations for Kessler_3 and Kessler_6 per Analysis_updated.Rmd
data_plan.sample_size: Raw N=766; analytic N computed at runtime after filters (expected ~600–700 based on non-missing patterns)
data_plan.access_details: Provided locally in /app/data as AlTammemi_Survey_deidentify.csv; no special access restrictions for replication.

qualification.explanation: This dataset contains the same core constructs as the original study: Kessler K10 psychological distress (10 items) and an ordinal measure of motivation for distance learning. It allows a conceptually faithful test of the focal claim (inverse association between severe distress and motivation) on an independent sample collected under similar conditions (COVID-era online survey).
qualification.similarity_to_original: Both use the K10 scale and its standard categorization thresholds (10-19 No; 20-24 Mild/Low; 25-29 Moderate; 30-50 Severe) and analyze the relationship with motivation for distance learning. Evidence: Original methods and thresholds described in post_registration.json (method.description, data.notes) and summarized in original_paper.pdf (PDF summary: K10 scoring and categories). The replication dataset includes Kessler_1..Kessler_10 and online_learning_1 columns (replication_data CSV headers), mirroring these constructs as shown in Analysis_updated.Rmd.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original sample: Jordanian university students recruited via Facebook/WhatsApp in May 2020, N=381 (post_registration.json: data.source, data.wave_or_subset; original_paper.pdf summary). Replication sample: Prolific panel participants targeted for student status, with data collected around 2021 (Analysis_updated.Rmd indicates Prolific recruitment, created timestamp filtering). Thus, country, sampling frame, and period differ. The original used SPSS v23.0, whereas the replication uses Python/statsmodels. The replication may also differ in exact operationalization of the regression model (ordered logit on motivation outcome) versus the original report’s multinomial logistic framing; we include both specifications to align with the Rmd logic.

data_plan.notes: Data columns include: Kessler_1..Kessler_10 (numeric 1–5), online_learning_1 (ordinal 1–4), created timestamp, session, demographic_1..4, participant_id. Following Analysis_updated.Rmd, we apply targeted imputations: set Kessler_3=1 if missing and Kessler_2==1; set Kessler_6=1 if missing and Kessler_5==1. We remove pre-collection test sessions by keeping rows with created > 2021-01-19 17:50:32. Motivation scale interpretation: higher values indicate higher motivation (median=3). No additional covariates are provided or specified for the focal test.

steps: Filter rows to created timestamp > 2021-01-19 17:50:32 to remove pre-collection tests (per Analysis_updated.Rmd).
Impute Kessler_3 to 1 if missing and Kessler_2==1; impute Kessler_6 to 1 if missing and Kessler_5==1 (per Analysis_updated.Rmd).
Compute Kessler_total as the sum of Kessler_1..Kessler_10; categorize into No (10–19), Low (20–24), Moderate (25–29), Severe (>=30).
Restrict to complete cases on Kessler_categories and online_learning_1.
Primary model: Ordered logistic regression with online_learning_1 as the ordinal outcome, predictors are dummy indicators for Low, Moderate, Severe distress (baseline: No Distress).
Extract odds ratios for distress levels, focusing on Severe vs No Distress. OR<1 indicates lower odds of higher motivation among severely distressed students.
Robustness: Multinomial logit with Kessler_categories as outcome (baseline No Distress) and online_learning_1 as predictor, extracting relative risk ratios.
Save model summaries and coefficients (including OR and 95% CI) to /app/data.
planned_method.models: Primary: Ordered logistic regression (proportional odds). Robustness: Multinomial logistic regression.
planned_method.outcome_variable: online_learning_1 (ordinal 1=lowest to 4=highest motivation for distance learning).
planned_method.independent_variables: Kessler psychological distress categories: Low, Moderate, Severe (baseline: No Distress).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Odds ratios for distress category indicators (especially Severe vs No Distress) from the ordered logistic model.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wald z-tests for coefficients; 95% confidence intervals.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion on required variables after targeted Kessler imputations (Kessler_3, Kessler_6 rules).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single focal contrast (Severe vs No Distress) emphasized; no multiple-testing correction applied.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-sided p<0.05 and OR<1 for Severe vs No Distress indicates replication consistent with original inverse relationship.

files.replication_data/run_replication__py.py: Entry point that runs the focal ordered logistic analysis (motivation outcome) and the robustness MNLogit specification (distress outcome). Reads from and writes to /app/data.
files.replication_data/analysis_dl_outcome__py: Focal replication analysis: filters by timestamp, applies Kessler imputations, computes Kessler_total and categories, fits an ordered logistic regression of online_learning_1 on Kessler categories (baseline No Distress), and writes JSON/CSV results to /app/data.
files.replication_data/analysis__py.py: Robustness analysis: same preprocessing as focal, fits MNLogit with Kessler_categories as outcome (baseline No Distress) and online_learning_1 as predictor, outputs ORs and CIs to /app/data.

codebase.notes: All IO strictly uses /app/data per run policy. The R Markdown logic was translated to Python; no original files were overwritten. The scripts depend on statsmodels for ordered and multinomial logistic models. Ensure that AlTammemi_Survey_deidentify.csv is available in /app/data.

analysis.instructions: 1) Place AlTammemi_Survey_deidentify.csv in ./data (mounted to /app/data). 2) Run: python replication_data/run_replication__py.py. 3) Inspect outputs: /app/data/results_ordinal_motivation.json and /app/data/results_ordinal_motivation_or.csv (focal), and /app/data/results_mnlogit.json and /app/data/results_mnlogit_or.csv (robustness).
analysis.comparison_metrics: Primary: Odds ratio for Severe vs No Distress affecting motivation (ordered logit). Replication success if OR<1 and statistically significant (95% CI excludes 1), aligning with original inverse effect (reported OR≈0.45, P<0.0001; initial_details.txt and original_paper.pdf). Secondary: Direction and significance in MNLogit RRRs consistent with inverse relationship.
